title: Fixing the Linked List Node Creation

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input a list of integers representing node values and the value of the new node to add.
statement.outputFormat: Modified linked list with the new node added.
statement.notes: This problem involves creating a new linked list node and inserting it at the correct position in the list.

examples.explanation: Add node with value 3 to the linked list [1, 2]

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: void addNode(ListNode* &head, int val) { if (!head) head = new ListNode(val); } };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public void addNode(ListNode head, int val) { if (head == null) head = new ListNode(val); } }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def add_node(self, head, val): if not head: head = ListNode(val);
starterCode.javascript: var addNode = function(head, val) { if (!head) head = new ListNode(val); };

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: void addNode(ListNode* &head, int val) { if (!head) head = new ListNode(val); else { head->val = val; }} };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public void addNode(ListNode head, int val) { if (head == null) head = new ListNode(val); else { head.val = val; }} }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def add_node(self, head, val): if not head: head = ListNode(val); else: head.val = val;
buggyStarterCode.javascript: var addNode = function(head, val) { if (!head) head = new ListNode(val); else { head.val = val; }};
